title: sempervivum transcaucasicum subsp. aghricum
intro: Sempervivum transcaucasicum subsp. aghricum, also known as Aghricum, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Sempervivum genus, characterized by its rosettes of thick, fleshy leaves. Traditionally, Aghricum has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting skin health. Aghricum is often prepared into herbal infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific study and validation.
